openIdConnect.description: OpenID Connect discovery for the same access tokens and scopes (runs:read / runs:write / runs:purge); the discovery URL is deployment-specific (placeholder shown).

mtls.description: Mutual-TLS client-certificate authentication, for zero-trust service/operator callers that prefer certificates over tokens. Mutual TLS carries no scopes, so the host maps the client certificate's identity to a capability tier (read/write/purge) out of band.

IdempotencyKeyHeader.name: Idempotency-Key
IdempotencyKeyHeader.description: An optional client-supplied idempotency key that makes starting a run idempotent (design §5.5): a repeated request carrying the same key returns the run the first request created rather than starting a duplicate, so a retried or redelivered fire (for example a durable schedule re-firing an occurrence after a runner recycle) starts the target at most once. Omit it for an ordinary one-shot start.

CredentialSourceNamePath.name: sourceName
CredentialSourceNamePath.description: The Arazzo source description name the credential authenticates calls to.

EnvironmentPath.name: environment
EnvironmentPath.description: The deployment environment the binding applies to.

StatusQuery.name: status
StatusQuery.description: Restrict to runs in this lifecycle status.

WorkflowIdQuery.name: workflowId
WorkflowIdQuery.description: Restrict to runs of this workflow.

LimitQuery.name: limit
LimitQuery.description: The maximum number of entries to return in this page (also the maximum to delete, for purge).

PageTokenQuery.name: pageToken
PageTokenQuery.description: An opaque continuation token from a previous page's `nextPageToken`, for paging.

GranteeQuery.name: grantee
GranteeQuery.description: An opaque URL-safe token identifying the grantee: base64url of a resolved grantee's JSON (a ResolvedGrantee object from a GET /identity/grantees result), round-tripped verbatim by the client.

CreatedAfterQuery.name: createdAfter
CreatedAfterQuery.description: Restrict to runs created at or after this instant (RFC 3339 date-time).

CreatedBeforeQuery.name: createdBefore
CreatedBeforeQuery.description: Restrict to runs created strictly before this instant (RFC 3339 date-time).

UpdatedAfterQuery.name: updatedAfter
UpdatedAfterQuery.description: Restrict to runs last updated at or after this instant (RFC 3339 date-time).

UpdatedBeforeQuery.name: updatedBefore
UpdatedBeforeQuery.description: Restrict to runs last updated strictly before this instant (RFC 3339 date-time).

TagQuery.name: tag
TagQuery.description: Restrict to runs carrying every supplied tag (repeat the parameter to require multiple, e.g. `?tag=tenant-42&tag=priority`).

CorrelationIdQuery.name: correlationId
CorrelationIdQuery.description: Restrict to runs with this telemetry correlation id (exact match), to pivot from a trace to its run.

OlderThanQuery.name: olderThan
OlderThanQuery.description: Reap terminal runs last updated strictly before this instant (RFC 3339 date-time).

BaseWorkflowIdPath.name: baseWorkflowId
BaseWorkflowIdPath.description: The base workflow id (without any version suffix).

AdministratorDigestPath.name: digest
AdministratorDigestPath.description: The stable identity digest of an administrator (from AdministratorGrant.digest) — the key for removing it.

AttachedSourceNamePath.name: name
AttachedSourceNamePath.description: The sourceDescriptions name the attachment satisfies.

ScenarioNamePath.name: scenarioName
ScenarioNamePath.description: The scenario's name (unique within the working copy).

SourceNamePath.name: name
SourceNamePath.description: The source's registered name (matches a workflow's sourceDescriptions entry).

VersionNumberPath.name: versionNumber
VersionNumberPath.description: The 1-based version number under the base workflow id.

CatalogSourceNamePath.name: sourceName
CatalogSourceNamePath.description: The name of a source document within the version's package (as listed in the version metadata's `sources` array).

CatalogSearchQuery.name: q
CatalogSearchQuery.description: Free-text filter matched against each version's title and description (case-insensitive contains).

CatalogBaseWorkflowIdQuery.name: baseWorkflowId
CatalogBaseWorkflowIdQuery.description: Restrict to versions of this base workflow id.

CatalogWorkflowIdPrefixQuery.name: workflowIdPrefix
CatalogWorkflowIdPrefixQuery.description: Restrict to versions whose versioned workflow id starts with this prefix (case-insensitive). Designed for type-ahead: it is an anchored prefix match (index-friendly) over the versioned workflow id, which begins with the base workflow id — so typing a base name matches all of its versions.

CatalogTagQuery.name: tag
CatalogTagQuery.description: Restrict to versions carrying every supplied tag (repeat to require multiple).

CatalogStatusQuery.name: status
CatalogStatusQuery.description: Restrict to versions in this lifecycle status.

CatalogOwnerQuery.name: owner
CatalogOwnerQuery.description: Restrict to versions whose owner name or email matches (case-insensitive contains).

CatalogDistinctWorkflowsQuery.name: distinctWorkflows
CatalogDistinctWorkflowsQuery.description: Collapse the result to one row per base workflow instead of one per version. When true, each page carries a single representative version per base workflow — the newest Active version, else the newest Obsolete, else the newest — and the page is keyset-ordered by base workflow id (the continuation cursor is the base id alone). A base workflow appears if any of its versions match the other filters, and its representative is the best-matching version. When omitted or false, one row per version is returned, keyset by base workflow id then version.

Forbidden.description: The caller may see the row but is not permitted to perform this action on it (row-level write authorization).

value.title: Action not permitted
value.detail: You do not have permission to modify run 'adopt-42'.

NotFound.description: No run with that id exists.

Conflict.description: The action does not apply in the run's current state, or the run is held by another owner (lease) or was changed concurrently.

notFaulted.summary: Resume requested for a run that is not faulted

value.title: Run is not resumable
value.detail: Run 'adopt-42' is Completed; only a Faulted run can be resumed.

leaseHeld.summary: Another owner holds the run's lease

value.title: Run is busy
value.detail: Run 'adopt-42' is currently held by another owner.

NextCatalogPage.description: Fetch the next page of catalog versions; absent in effect when nextPageToken is null.

CatalogPackage.description: Download this version's full document package as a binary stream.

CatalogWorkflow.description: Fetch just this version's Arazzo workflow document.

NextPage.description: Fetch the next page of runs; absent in effect when nextPageToken is null (the last page).

ResumeRun.description: Resume this run from its last checkpoint (only applies if it is faulted).

CountResult.description: A bounded count of matching items for a list, returned by the '/count' endpoints — no rows are materialised (for work badges and list footers). When 'capped' is true, 'count' is the server's cap rather than the true total, so it should be rendered as e.g. '100+'.

count.description: The number of matching items, bounded by the server's cap.

capped.description: True when the true total meets or exceeds the cap, so 'count' is the cap, not the true total.

AccessGrantsOverview.description: The bounded who-can-do-what summary for one grantee (design §6.1): its identity, the capability scopes its bindings confer, and the environments it administers. The unbounded lists — the bindings that grant reach, the workflows it administers, and the credentials its runs may use — are the keyset-paged sub-resources GET /access/grants/{reach,administered,credentials}.

capabilities.description: The capability scopes the matched bindings confer, resolved exactly as the runtime resolves them (unexpired bindings only; an eligible-only binding confers eligibility, not an active scope). One entry per scope; active dominates eligible. Rolled up over all of the grantee's matched bindings (not paged).

administersEnvironments.description: The environments the grantee administers (membership; a grantee administers a bounded handful, so this is not paged).

AccessGrantsReachPage.description: One keyset page of the security bindings whose claim matches the grantee's identity; each carries its per-verb read/write/purge reach. Delete a binding to revoke that access.

nextPageToken.description: The keyset cursor for the next page, absent on the last page.

AccessGrantsAdministeredPage.description: One keyset page of the base workflows the grantee administers (membership: an administrator identity contained in the grantee's identity).

nextPageToken.description: The keyset cursor for the next page, absent on the last page.

AccessGrantsCredentialsPage.description: One keyset page of the source-credential bindings the grantee's runs may use — the credentials whose usage grant is scoped to the grantee's identity (a label-superset over the binding's usage grant; shared deployment-wide bindings are omitted).

nextPageToken.description: The keyset cursor for the next page, absent on the last page.

AccessGrantsCapability.description: One capability scope a grantee's matched bindings confer (design §16.5): active (held now) or eligible-only (a PIM eligibility the grantee may self-elevate to).

eligible.description: True when the scope is eligible-only (self-elevation required); false when it is held actively.

expiresAt.description: When the last conferring binding expires; absent when a conferring binding never expires.

AccessGrantsAdministeredEnvironment.description: An environment a grantee administers (a link target for the environments panel), enriched with a summary of the environment so the row reads without a detail fetch.

displayName.description: The environment's human-facing display name, when set.

allowsDraftRuns.description: Whether working-copy drafts may execute here as debug runs (workflow-designer design §18). Absent means the environment could not be read (out of the reader's reach or since deleted); false means it does not allow draft runs.

availability.description: A bounded count of the workflow versions available in this environment (the count-API pattern; rendered e.g. '12' or '100+'). Absent when the availability store is not configured.

AccessGrantsAdministeredWorkflow.description: A base workflow a grantee administers (a link target for the catalog), enriched with a summary of its representative catalog version so the row reads without a detail fetch.

title.description: The representative version's title (display name), when set.

latestVersion.description: The representative catalog version number (the one the catalog surfaces for this workflow). Absent when no version is readable.

status.description: The representative version's lifecycle status (e.g. 'Available', 'Obsolete'), when a version is readable.

owner.description: The representative version's owner name, when a version is readable and an owner is set.

AccessGrantsCredentialUsage.description: A source-credential binding a grantee's runs may use, keyed by (source name, environment).

environment.description: The environment the binding is scoped to.

WorkflowRunStatus.description: A run's lifecycle status. `Faulted` is terminal-but-recoverable (awaiting remediation); `Completed` and `Cancelled` are terminal.

WorkflowWaitKind.description: What a suspended run is waiting for.

dueAt.description: When a timer wait becomes due (set for `Timer`).

channel.description: The channel a message wait listens on (set for `Message`).

correlationId.description: The correlation id a message wait matches, if any.

attempt.description: The attempt count at the fault.

WorkflowRunSummary.description: A run as projected into the visibility index (one entry in a page).

environment.description: The deployment environment the run is pinned to (design §5.5): its credential set, and the runners it can be dispatched to.

errorType.description: The fault error, if the run is/was faulted.

correlationId.description: The run-wide telemetry correlation id set at creation (the value stamped on the run's `corvus.arazzo.correlation_id` spans), if any.

tags.description: The free-form tags applied to the run at creation.

WorkflowRunPage.description: A page of runs matching a visibility query.

nextPageToken.description: An opaque token to fetch the next page, or null/absent if this is the last page.

WorkflowRunSteps.description: A run's recorded step journal, projected from its authoritative checkpoint: each step the run executed, in execution order, with its outcome, attempt, time window, and recorded outputs (ADR 0050). A run whose checkpoint predates the journal reports only the steps that recorded outputs, with no status or timing (nothing is invented).

steps.description: The recorded steps, in the order the run executed them.

truncated.description: True when the journal was capped and its oldest entries were dropped (a long-running or looping run, ADR 0050); the retained steps are the most recent.

WorkflowRunStepRecord.description: One recorded step in a run's journal: its id, its outcome, the attempt it settled on, the time window it executed in, and the outputs value the checkpoint stored for it (ADR 0050). Status and timing are attested for runs journaled under ADR 0050; a step from a checkpoint that predates the journal reports only its id and outputs, and nothing is invented. When the caller may read the journal but not this step's payload (a version classified sensitive, or a field marked sensitive in the output schema, without the stronger grant), only the outputs are redacted: `redacted` is true and the outputs are withheld, while the non-sensitive id, status, attempt, and timing are preserved.

status.description: The step's recorded outcome (ADR 0050). Absent for a step from a checkpoint that predates the journal.

attempt.description: The attempt the step settled on (consistent with a fault's attempt number). Absent for a pre-journal step.

startedAt.description: When the step's (final) execution began. Absent for a pre-journal step.

endedAt.description: When the step's execution ended. Absent for a pre-journal step.

outputs.description: The step's recorded outputs value, verbatim from the checkpoint. Absent when the step recorded none, or withheld when `redacted` is true.

redacted.description: True when this step's outputs were withheld from the caller because they are classified sensitive and the caller lacks the stronger grant (write reach on the run). Only the outputs payload is withheld; the step's id, status, attempt, and timing are still disclosed (non-disclosing of the payload only).

WorkflowRunDetail.description: A run's management detail, read from its authoritative checkpoint.

environment.description: The deployment environment the run is pinned to (design §5.5): its credential set, and the runners it can be dispatched to.

cursor.description: The state-machine index of the next step to run.

updatedAt.description: When the run's checkpoint was last written; absent on a run checkpointed before this stamp existed.

wait.description: Why the run is suspended, if it is.

fault.description: The fault record if the run is (or was) faulted.

etag.description: The optimistic-concurrency token the checkpoint was read at.

correlationId.description: The run-wide telemetry correlation id set at creation (the value stamped on the run's `corvus.arazzo.correlation_id` spans), if any.

tags.description: The free-form tags applied to the run at creation.

ResumeRequest.description: How to resume a faulted run — a union on `mode`. Each form carries only the fields its mode needs; the `const` mode discriminates the variant.

RetryFaultedStepResume.description: Re-execute from the last checkpoint — the faulted step. The common case.

RewindResume.description: Reset the cursor to an earlier step and re-run forward from there.

SkipResume.description: Advance the cursor past the faulted step, optionally recording operator-supplied outputs for it.

targetCursor.description: The cursor to resume at (defaults to the faulted cursor + 1).

skipOutputs.description: The operator-supplied outputs to record for the skipped step so downstream references resolve. Any JSON value.

StatePatchResume.description: Apply an RFC 6902 JSON Patch to the run's context object { "inputs": …, "stepOutputs": { … } }, then retry the faulted step.

CancelRequest.description: A request to cancel a run.

reason.description: An operator-supplied reason for the cancellation (recorded for audit). Must be non-empty.

purgedCount.description: The number of runs or versions deleted.

ValidationTarget.description: Identifies the schema within a version's package to validate a value against.

kind.description: Which schema: a workflow's inputs, a step's request body, a step's response body, or a step's outputs object.

workflowId.description: The (versioned) workflow id; defaults to the version's workflow when omitted.

stepId.description: The step id. Required for requestBody, responseBody and stepOutputs.

status.description: The response status code for a responseBody target; defaults to the success (lowest 2xx, else default) response.

ValidationRequest.description: A request to validate a JSON value against a target schema in a version's package.

value.description: The JSON value to validate (any JSON type).

instancePath.description: A JSON Pointer to the failing location within the posted value.

message.description: A human-readable description of the failure.

keyword.description: The JSON Schema keyword that failed, when known.

schemaLocation.description: The evaluation location within the schema that produced the failure, when known.

ValidationResult.description: The outcome of validating a value against a schema.

valid.description: Whether the value conformed to the schema.

WorkflowRunAccepted.description: Acknowledges a triggered run that will execute asynchronously and durably on a hosting runner.

runId.description: The id of the created run; poll the runs endpoints to observe its progress.

workflowId.description: The versioned workflow id the run executes.

ScheduleCreate.description: A request to create a durable schedule (#896): the target version, the environment to fire it in, and the cron cadence.

scheduleId.description: The schedule's stable, globally-unique identity. Re-creating the same id is idempotent.

environment.description: The deployment environment the schedule and its fired runs are pinned to; a runner serving it must advertise scheduling.

targetBaseWorkflowId.description: The base workflow id of the version to fire.

targetVersionNumber.description: The version number of the workflow to fire.

cron.description: The cron expression driving occurrences (5-field, or 6-field when includeSeconds is true).

timeZone.description: The IANA time zone id the cadence is expressed in.

includeSeconds.description: Whether the cron expression carries a leading seconds field.

targetInputs.description: The inputs passed to each fired run of the target (validated against the version's baked inputs schema).

Schedule.description: A durable schedule (#896): the projection of a scheduler run showing its cadence, target, and firing state.

environment.description: The deployment environment the schedule is pinned to.

targetBaseWorkflowId.description: The base workflow id of the version fired.

timeZone.description: The IANA time zone id the cadence is expressed in.

includeSeconds.description: Whether the cron expression carries a leading seconds field.

nextOccurrence.description: The next instant the schedule will fire, if any (absent for a bounded cron that has no further occurrences).

lastFiredOccurrence.description: The most recent occurrence fired (the watermark), if the schedule has fired at least once.

nextPageToken.description: An opaque continuation token for the next page, absent on the last page.

PageLimit.description: A page size: the maximum number of items to return (or delete, for purge).

TagList.description: A set of free-form tags (AND-matched when used as a filter).

OutputsSensitivity.description: How sensitive a catalog version's step-output payloads are for disclosure (design §14), distinct from row-visibility security tags. 'standard' (the default when absent) reads the step journal at the runs:outputs:read tier; 'sensitive' redacts the whole journal from callers who hold only that tier and requires the stronger grant (write reach on the run).

CatalogOwner.description: The accountable governance owner of a workflow, for integration with governance tooling.

name.description: The owning person or team's display name.

url.description: An optional link (e.g. a runbook or owning-team page).

CatalogPackage.description: The logical content of a workflow document package: the Arazzo workflow plus its referenced source documents. The package is transferred and stored as an opaque ZIP archive (manifest.json + workflow.json + sources/<name>.json); this schema documents that archive's logical content. Only the catalog-relevant fields of the contained documents are typed; additional properties are permitted, so any Arazzo/OpenAPI/AsyncAPI version validates and the package stays a version-agnostic input to code generation.

sources.description: The referenced source documents (OpenAPI/AsyncAPI), keyed by the workflow's sourceDescriptions name.

CatalogPackageWorkflow.description: An Arazzo workflow document. The catalog-relevant fields are typed; additional properties are permitted.

info.description: The document info; the catalog projects title and description (falling back to summary).

sourceDescriptions.description: The source documents the workflow references.

workflows.description: The workflows in the document; the catalog uses the first workflow's id as the base id.

CatalogPackageSourceDescription.description: A named source description; the catalog surfaces the name and type.

CatalogPackageWorkflowEntry.description: A workflow within the Arazzo document; the catalog reads its id.

CatalogSourceRef.description: A reference to one source document within a version's package, as projected into the metadata.

name.description: The source name (the workflow's sourceDescriptions name), used to address the document.

type.description: The source document kind (e.g. `openapi`, `asyncapi`), if declared by the workflow.

CatalogSecurityTag.description: A non-internal security label (KVP) on a catalog version for row authorization (§14.2) — a reach label rules match on, e.g. domain=payments; a run inherits its version's labels. Distinct from free-form display `tags`, and from environment/source/credential `managementTags` (which scope who may MANAGE a row). Internal/reserved-prefix tags (e.g. tenant) are deployment-owned and never set here — see the operation descriptions.

CatalogMetadataPatch.description: A partial update of a version's mutable governance metadata; omitted fields are left unchanged. Closed (additionalProperties: false) so an unknown field is rejected with 400 rather than silently ignored. `securityTags` sets the version's NON-internal reach labels (§14.2) — a governed edit for a workflow administrator; the reserved internal-tag prefix is rejected (400), since internal tags (e.g. the deployment tenant) stay deployment-stamped and immutable.

securityTags.description: Replace the version's non-internal security tags (§14.2 reach labels). The reserved internal-tag prefix is rejected (400); internal tags remain deployment-owned.

outputsSensitivity.description: Classify this version's step-output payloads (design §14). 'sensitive' redacts the whole step journal from callers who hold only runs:outputs:read; reading it then needs the stronger grant (write reach on the run).

CatalogVersionSummary.description: A catalog version as projected into the searchable index (one entry in a page).

workflowId.description: The versioned workflow id runs execute under.

securityTags.description: The version's non-internal security tags (§14.2) — the reach labels row-security rules match on. Internal/reserved-prefix tags are deployment-owned and stripped from this view. A run inherits its version's security tags.

outputsSensitivity.description: How sensitive this version's step-output payloads are (design §14). Absent means 'standard'. 'sensitive' redacts the step journal from callers below the stronger grant.

sources.description: The source documents in this version's package, addressable individually via the source endpoint.

runnable.description: Whether this version carries a compiled workflow executor assembly (downloadable via the executor endpoint) that an execution host can run.

hash.description: The SHA-256 of the canonical package, hex-encoded.

evidence.description: The publish-evidence summary (§4.6), projected from the package's metadata/evidence.json. Present only on the single-version detail and only for versions published with scenario evidence; index pages omit it. The full per-scenario record is served by the evidence endpoint.

CatalogPage.description: A page of catalog versions matching a search.

nextPageToken.description: An opaque token to fetch the next page; absent on the last page.

RunnerHostedVersion.description: A catalog version a runner currently hosts.

hash.description: The content hash of the hosted version's package.

loaded.description: Whether the runner has the version loaded and ready to execute.

RunnerSecurityTag.description: A security label (KVP) scoping a runner for row authorization (§14.2) — e.g. tenant=acme.

Runner.description: A workflow runner process registered with the control plane.

runnerId.description: The stable identifier of the runner process.

environment.description: The single deployment environment this runner serves (design §5.5); the runner is dispatchable only for runs targeting it and inherits its reach.

reachTags.description: The runner's row-security reach (§14.2), stamped from the serving environment's managementTags at registration; a tenant sees only runners serving its environments. Absent on a runner serving an unscoped environment.

address.description: An optional address (for example a base URI) at which the runner can be reached.

lastSeenAt.description: The instant of the runner's most recent heartbeat.

maxConcurrency.description: The maximum number of runs the runner will execute concurrently.

transports.description: The transport identifiers the runner supports.

hostedVersions.description: The catalog versions the runner currently hosts.

servesSchedules.description: Whether this runner claims durable schedule runs (#896) pinned to its environment — the built-in scheduler engine seam. Scheduling in an environment requires at least one runner here that advertises this; the control plane surfaces per-environment scheduling capability from it. Absent means false.

RunnerRegistrationRequest.description: A runner's self-description when it registers to serve a deployment environment (design §5.5/§16.4). The runner presents only what it knows about itself; the control plane stamps the rest: the environment (from the path), the runner's reach tags (from the environment's managementTags — never trusted from the runner), the last-seen instant (now), and the trusted machine principal (from the authenticated token). The registration enters Pending and is dispatchable only once an administrator of the environment authorizes it.

runnerId.description: The stable identifier of the runner process. Self-chosen; the trust anchor is the authenticated machine principal, not this id — a registration presenting a principal that differs from the one already bound to this runnerId is refused (409).

address.description: An optional address (for example a base URI) at which the runner can be reached.

maxConcurrency.description: The maximum number of runs the runner will execute concurrently.

transports.description: The transport identifiers the runner supports.

hostedVersions.description: The catalog versions the runner currently hosts.

hostsDraftRuns.description: Whether this runner claims §18 draft runs pinned to its environment (the working-copy debug-run engine seam). Absent means false; meaningful only on runners serving development-class environments.

servesSchedules.description: Whether this runner claims durable schedule runs (#896) pinned to its environment — the built-in scheduler engine seam. A schedule is a durable run of the reserved scheduler workflow, so scheduling in an environment requires at least one runner here that advertises this. Absent means false; the control plane surfaces per-environment scheduling capability from it, and refuses to create a schedule in an environment no runner serves.

RunnerPage.description: A keyset page of the workflow runners currently registered with the control plane, ordered by runnerId.

nextPageToken.description: An opaque token to fetch the next page, or null/absent if this is the last page.

EnvironmentRunnerAuthorizationView.description: A runner's authorization to serve a deployment environment (design §5.5): keyed by (environment, runnerId), governed by the environment's administrators. A runner enters Pending on registration and is dispatchable only once Authorized. Quarantined temporarily excludes a faulted runner from new dispatch while its in-flight runs drain, and it is reinstated to Authorized without re-registering. Revoked permanently removes a compromised runner and fences its in-flight work; returning it requires a deliberate re-authorization.

runnerId.description: The runner the authorization applies to.

status.description: The authorization's lifecycle state; only Authorized is dispatchable (Pending, Quarantined, and Revoked are all excluded from dispatch).

reason.description: An optional note recorded with the most recent decision.

createdBy.description: The actor that created the record (the runner self-registering enters Pending).

principal.description: The trusted machine principal that proved ownership of this runnerId by authenticating its registration (design §16.4): the authorization binds to this verified identity rather than to a self-asserted runnerId. Absent on a row an administrator pre-authorized by runnerId before any runner has registered.

createdAt.description: When the record was created (the runner first registered for the environment).

decidedBy.description: The environment administrator that last authorized or revoked the runner, if decided.

decidedAt.description: When the runner was last authorized or revoked, if decided.

EnvironmentRunnerAuthorizationList.description: A keyset page of runner authorizations, ordered by runnerId (for an environment) or by (environment, runnerId) (for the approver inbox).

nextPageToken.description: An opaque token to fetch the next page, or null/absent if this is the last page.

RunnerAuthorizationDecisionNote.description: An optional note recorded with an authorize, quarantine, reinstate, or revoke decision.

reason.description: An optional human-readable justification for the decision.

VerbGrant.description: A per-verb grant in a binding: either unrestricted (full reach — the operator escape) or the conjunction of the named rules. The default (unrestricted false, empty ruleNames) grants nothing for the verb.

unrestricted.description: When true the verb is unrestricted (full reach); ruleNames is ignored.

ruleNames.description: The rule names ANDed for the verb.

expression.description: The rule text in the security-rule grammar.

SecurityRuleUpdate.description: Replacement content for an existing security rule.

expression.description: The rule text in the security-rule grammar.

SecurityRuleList.description: A keyset page of security rules, ordered by name.

nextPageToken.description: An opaque token to fetch the next page, or null/absent if this is the last page.

SecurityOrdering.description: An ordered tag dimension: one dimension's labels in ascending order, the ranking an ordered rule comparison (<, <=, >, >=) uses.

labels.description: The dimension's labels from lowest to highest rank.

SecurityOrderingList.description: The deployment's configured ordered tag dimensions (design §14.2).

SecurityBindingWrite.description: The content of a claim→rule binding (create or replace).

claimType.description: The principal claim type this binding keys on ('*' matches any authenticated principal).

claimValue.description: The required claim value; omit to match any value of claimType.

additionalClauses.description: Extra identity-dimension clauses ANDed with the primary claimType/claimValue clause to form a tag-set selector: the binding applies only to a caller whose canonical identity contains every clause (the primary and each of these), so a per-person reach grant can pin {sub, iss} and match one issuer's principal rather than any. Omit or empty for a legacy single-clause binding.

SecurityBindingClause.description: One identity-dimension clause of a binding's tag-set selector: the caller's canonical identity must carry this dimension (and value, when pinned).

dimension.description: The operator-facing identity dimension (the sys: prefix stripped) the caller's identity must carry.

value.description: The value the dimension must equal; omit to match any value of the dimension.

additionalClauses.description: Extra identity-dimension clauses ANDed with the primary claimType/claimValue clause to form a tag-set selector: the binding applies only to a caller whose canonical identity contains every clause. Absent or empty for a legacy single-clause binding.

scopes.description: The capability scopes the binding confers on matching principals (design §16.5), if any.

expiresAt.description: When the binding expires (a §16.5.2 time-boxed grant); absent for a non-expiring binding.

eligibleOnly.description: True for a binding that records eligibility only (a PIM assignment the principal may self-elevate to): it confers nothing active.

SecurityBindingList.description: A keyset page of claim→rule bindings, ordered by (order, id).

nextPageToken.description: An opaque token to fetch the next page, or null/absent if this is the last page.

WorkspaceSecurityTag.description: A security label (KVP) scoping a working copy for row authorization (§14.2).

WorkingCopyDocument.description: The Arazzo document under edit, stored and returned verbatim. The control plane validates it on demand, never silently rewrites it.

WorkingCopyDesignerState.description: Designer UI state (node positions and similar) — a sibling of the document, never written into it and never packaged (workflow-designer design §5.2).

WorkingCopy.description: A designer working copy: the mutable Arazzo document (plus designer UI state) under development. Saving never mints a catalog version; publish does.

baseWorkflowId.description: Provenance: the catalog base workflow this working copy was opened from, if any.

basedOnVersion.description: Provenance: the catalog version the document was copied from, if any.

managementTags.description: Security tags scoping who may MANAGE and SEE this working copy (§14.2).

etag.description: The optimistic-concurrency token; saves present the etag they read and conflict (409) when stale.

WorkingCopySummary.description: A working copy without its document/designer state: the list projection.

etag.description: The optimistic-concurrency token; saves present the etag they read and conflict (409) when stale.

WorkingCopyCreate.description: Creates a working copy: from a supplied document, from a catalog version (fromBaseWorkflowId + fromVersionNumber — copies that version's workflow document; the carry-over), or blank (a minimal Arazzo skeleton). Supplying both a document and a from-version is rejected (400).

name.description: A display name; defaults to the document's first workflowId, or 'untitled'.

fromBaseWorkflowId.description: Open from this catalog base workflow…

fromVersionNumber.description: …at this version. Required when fromBaseWorkflowId is supplied (latest-version defaulting arrives with scenario carry-over).

managementTags.description: Non-internal reach labels; the deployment stamps the creator's internal tenant tag and rejects a set the creator's reach does not admit.

WorkingCopyUpdate.description: A save: replaces the document (and optionally the name/designer state) of an existing working copy. expectedEtag is the etag the client read; a stale save conflicts (409) rather than clobbering a collaborator's work. Provenance, managementTags, and created-* audit fields are immutable.

name.description: A replacement display name; absent leaves it unchanged.

gitBinding.description: A replacement Git binding (§4.7); absent leaves the stored binding unchanged.

expectedEtag.description: The etag the client read (409 when stale).

WorkingCopyList.description: A page of working copies, ordered by id. Entries omit the document and designer state.

nextPageToken.description: An opaque token to fetch the next page, or null/absent if this is the last page.

WorkflowDiagnostic.description: One positioned validation finding over a working copy's Arazzo document: where (a JSON Pointer), how bad (severity), what family of problem (category — 'schema' for JSON-Schema conformance findings; semantic slugs like 'goto-target', 'depends-on', 'criterion-syntax', 'criterion-type', 'expression-syntax', 'component-reference', 'duplicate-id', 'reachability'), and a human-readable message.

severity.description: error = cannot run correctly as written; warning = suspect but runnable; info = advisory.

category.description: The stable finding family (kebab-case slug) the UI groups and filters by.

instancePath.description: A JSON Pointer to the finding's location within the document.

message.description: A human-readable description of the finding.

schemaLocation.description: For schema-conformance findings: the evaluation location within the Arazzo schema that produced the failure.

GitBinding.title: GitBinding
GitBinding.description: Binds a working copy to a repository branch (workflow-designer design §4.7): the document — and optionally its spec files and scenario files — round-trips with pull/commit. Branch-per-working-copy is the natural multi-author flow.

branch.description: The working branch; a pull request opens FROM this branch.

path.description: The Arazzo document's path in the repository.

specPaths.description: sourceDescriptions name → repository path, for the spec files that round-trip with the document (each pulls in as an inline attachment).

scenariosDir.description: The directory holding one <name>.scenario.json per scenario (the §4.5 CI layout).

GitCommitResult.title: GitCommitResult
GitCommitResult.description: What a commit wrote: one entry per file, plus the pull request when one was opened. The commits are authored as the signed-in user's GitHub-held git identity — the control plane composes no author/committer (§4.7).

GitHubAuthStart.title: GitHubAuthStart
GitHubAuthStart.description: The begin-auth handshake: navigate the browser to authorizeUrl; the state round-trips through GitHub to the callback, where it identifies the principal (workflow-designer design §4.7).

authorizeUrl.description: The GitHub App authorize URL to navigate to.

state.description: The single-use, principal-bound state embedded in authorizeUrl.

GitHubStatus.title: GitHubStatus
GitHubStatus.description: The calling principal's GitHub session: the signed-in identity and, when connected, a first page of the repositories the user can reach (most recently pushed first), seeding pickers. Reach is the OAuth model's: whatever the signed-in user can see — any visible repository stays addressable directly by owner/repo, listed here or not.

repositories.description: A first page of the user's reachable repositories (a picker seed, not the full reach).

GitHubRepositoryList.title: GitHubRepositoryList
GitHubRepositoryList.description: A pickers-sized page of repositories matching a typeahead query.

GitHubRepository.title: GitHubRepository
GitHubRepository.description: A repository reachable through the brokered session.

GitHubBranch.title: GitHubBranch
GitHubBranch.description: A branch head reachable through the brokered session.

GitHubBranchList.title: GitHubBranchList
GitHubBranchList.description: A repository's branches plus its default branch — the Git dialog's branch picker.

GitHubCommit.title: GitHubCommit
GitHubCommit.description: One commit in the brokered history — enough to browse, compare, and roll back.

GitHubCommitList.title: GitHubCommitList
GitHubCommitList.description: One page of a repository's commit history, newest first — the Git pane's history browser.

GitHubBranchCreate.title: GitHubBranchCreate
GitHubBranchCreate.description: Creates a branch from a base branch's head (default: the repository's default branch).

from.description: The base branch (default: the default branch).

GitHubBrowseResult.title: GitHubBrowseResult
GitHubBrowseResult.description: A proxied contents read: a directory lists its entries; a file carries its content.

GitHubFile.title: GitHubFile
GitHubFile.description: A file's content, base64-encoded (the GitHub contents shape).

EvidenceSuite.title: EvidenceSuite
EvidenceSuite.description: The suite verdict: how many scenarios ran at publish and how many passed.

PublishEvidenceSummary.title: PublishEvidenceSummary
PublishEvidenceSummary.description: The evidence badge's data (design §4.6): when the version was published and the suite verdict, without the per-scenario detail.

PublishEvidence.title: PublishEvidence
PublishEvidence.description: The server-attested record written into the package at publish (design §4.6).

Scenario.title: Scenario
Scenario.description: One deterministic test scenario (design §4.2), stored with the working copy and published into the package. v1 deferrals, deliberate: per-request match criteria on mocks and response delays (the mock transport scripts sequences per route), and trigger scheduling (`at`) — triggers deliver when the run suspends on their channel.

mocks.description: Per source-operation scripting; a run resolves each (source, operationId) to its route through the working copy's attached surfaces.

expect.title: ScenarioExpectations
expect.description: Assertions over the run, in the criterion grammar where a condition applies.

outputs.description: Criteria evaluated against the completed run's workflow outputs ($outputs).

attempts.description: The exact number of executions (retries and revisits included).

passed.description: Every expectation held (a scenario with no expectations passes when the run completes).

SimulateRequest.title: SimulateRequest
SimulateRequest.description: One deterministic simulation command: the scenario setup (inline in v1; named stored scenarios arrive with the scenario store), an optional stop condition, and an optional budget. The same request shape backs run, step, run-to-here, and re-run-after-edit — only `until` differs.

workflowId.description: Which of the document's workflows to simulate (default: the first).

scenario.title: InlineScenario
scenario.description: The deterministic setup: workflow inputs, scripted mock responses, message triggers, and the virtual-clock policy.

mocks.description: Scripted responses for the mock API transport, keyed by method + path template. Repeated entries for one route form a sequence (the last repeats). Unmocked routes return 404.

body.description: The response body value, if any.

triggers.description: Messages delivered when the run suspends waiting on a channel, in order.

channel.description: The channel the waiting step listens on.

correlationId.description: The correlation id, when the wait is correlated.

clock.title: ClockPolicy
clock.description: The virtual-clock policy. Nothing ever waits in real time.

autoAdvance.description: Advance the virtual clock to each timer's due moment and resume automatically. When false the trace ends suspended at the first timer with its due time.

until.title: StopCondition
until.description: Where to stop the replay. Omitted: run to completion (or fault/suspend/budget).

beforeStepId.description: Pause before this step executes (the paused context is the state the step will see). A **scoped step path**: a bare `stepId` targets a root step; a slash-joined `parent/child/...` path (each segment a `stepId`, outermost first) targets a step inside a sub-workflow. A bare id never matches a sub-workflow's step.

occurrence.description: Which arrival at `beforeStepId` pauses, for steps reached repeatedly (goto/retry loops). Occurrences are counted cumulatively by the root run.

breakpoints.description: Pause before ANY of these steps executes (first arrival wins over `beforeStepId` when both match).

budget.title: SimulationBudget
budget.description: Safety limits (§8.3). The server clamps to its own maxima.

wallClockMs.description: The real-time execution cap in milliseconds (default 10000).

DebugRunPause.description: Where a debug run pauses (workflow-designer design §18): after every step (single-stepping) and/or before named steps (breakpoints). Absent — the run proceeds to completion, a wait, or a fault.

afterEachStep.description: Pause after every executed step — the dock's single-step.

beforeSteps.description: Pause before these steps execute — breakpoints. Each entry is a **scoped step path**: a bare `stepId` for a root step, or a slash-joined `parent/child/...` path for a step inside a sub-workflow (a bare id never matches a sub-workflow's step).

DebugRunStart.description: Start a debug run of the working copy's stored document in an environment that allows drafts (workflow-designer design §18).

workflowId.description: The workflow within the document to run.

environment.description: The target environment; it must set allowsDraftRuns and the caller must be entitled to run there.

inputs.description: The workflow's inputs, validated against its own inputs schema.

DebugRunResume.description: Advance a paused, suspended, or faulted debug run. A bare resume continues to the next pause point; `action` applies the SAME resume union the runs view uses (Skip with outputs = the dock's step-over; Rewind deliberately re-executes real side effects).

DebugRunMessageInjection.description: A message injected into a debug run suspended on an AsyncAPI receive (workflow-designer design §18 / §3.3). Debug-only: the payload is delivered straight to the awaiting run, standing in for the real publisher.

channel.description: The AsyncAPI channel the run awaits (from the suspended run's wait descriptor).

correlationId.description: The correlation the receive step matches on, when the channel carries several conversations; omit to match the channel alone.

payload.description: The message payload delivered to the awaiting receive step. Any JSON value; typed from the channel's message schema in the designer's inject form.

DebugRun.description: A debug run: a durable, forward-only run of a working-copy draft in a development-class environment (workflow-designer design §18). The trace grows as the run advances and uses the same shape simulation emits — capture-to-scenario replays it offline with full time travel.

status.description: paused = at a pause point (the dock's controls apply); suspended = a real wait (message/time); the rest are terminal except running.

cursor.description: The number of steps executed so far — the position the dock scrubs to.

documentEtag.description: The working-copy etag the run started from — the audited draft identity.

SimulationTrace.title: SimulationTrace
SimulationTrace.description: The complete structured trace up to the stop condition — the canvas overlay, context explorer, trace viewer, and time-travel scrubber all render from this one payload with no further calls. Recursive: a sub-workflow step's `subTrace` is itself a SimulationTrace.

workflowId.description: The sub-workflow's `workflowId`, present only on a nested `subTrace` and absent on the root trace. Marks this trace as one sub-workflow invocation's records within its parent; the client's step-into ascent stops where it becomes absent.

outcome.description: How the replay ended: ran to completion; faulted; paused at the stop condition; suspended awaiting a timer (autoAdvance false) or an undelivered message; or the step budget ran out.

pausedBefore.description: The step the replay paused before, when `outcome` is `paused`. A **scoped step path**: a bare `stepId` for a root step, or a slash-joined `parent/child/...` path (each segment a `stepId`, outermost first) when the stop fired inside a sub-workflow.

outputs.description: The workflow outputs value, when `outcome` is `completed`.

wait.title: SimulationWait
wait.description: What the run is waiting on, when `outcome` is `suspended`.

dueAt.description: The virtual-clock due moment, for a timer wait.

channel.description: The awaited channel, for a message wait.

steps.description: One record per executed step, in execution order — a step reached repeatedly appears repeatedly.

status.description: The step's terminal status, or — on a sub-workflow parent step whose child did not finish — `paused` (a scoped stop fired inside the child) or `suspended` (the child is awaiting a timer or message).

attempt.description: The retry attempt this record describes (0 = first try).

outputs.description: The step's extracted outputs value, if any.

requests.description: The request/response pairs this step exchanged with the mock transport, in order.

path.description: The resolved path (parameters substituted, query included).

requestBody.description: The request payload value, if one was sent.

responseBody.description: The mock's response body value, if any.

successCriteria.description: The step's success-criterion truth table, re-evaluated against the captured deterministic context (operand-level detail is a documented deferral).

actionTaken.title: ActionTaken
actionTaken.description: The routing decision inferred for this step: the first action whose criteria all passed, or the implicit fall-through/completion.

name.description: The action's name, for an explicit action.

target.description: The goto/retry-from target stepId, when one applies.

skipped.description: True when a Skip resume skipped this step rather than executing it (the durable debug-run Skip protocol). Absent or false for an executed step.

subTrace.description: For a step bound to another workflow (`workflowId`), the nested trace of that sub-workflow's execution — the same shape as the root trace, carrying its own `workflowId`. Powers step-into; absent for non-sub-workflow steps.

clockAdvances.description: Every virtual-clock advance, in order (timer auto-advances).

stepsExecuted.description: The total step executions (retries and revisits included) — what the budget counts.

WorkingCopyDiagnostics.description: The outcome of validating a working copy's Arazzo document: JSON-Schema conformance against the Arazzo schema for the document's declared version, plus document-local semantic checks (goto/retry targets, dependsOn references, criterion syntax via the runtime's own compiler, runtime-expression syntax, reusable component references, duplicate ids, reachability). Source-dependent checks (unresolved operationId/operationPath/channelPath) arrive with attached sources. Warnings do not fail validation — valid means no errors.

valid.description: Whether the document is free of error-severity findings (warnings/infos may still be present).

diagnostics.description: Every finding, in document order (empty when clean).

AttachedSource.description: One source attached to a working copy under a sourceDescriptions name (workflow-designer design §4.1): a registry reference (resolved at read time against the caller-reachable registered source) or an inline document (upload/fetch result, stored on the working copy so it resolves like a package). The list never returns documents; the operations endpoint projects them.

name.description: The sourceDescriptions name this attachment satisfies.

kind.description: registry = resolves a registered source by name at read time; inline = the document is stored on the working copy.

sourceName.description: registry only: the registered source's name.

type.description: The source type (openapi | asyncapi | arazzo | jsonschema), recorded for inline attachments and echoed from the registry otherwise. A jsonschema attachment is referenced by inputs-schema external $ref (schemas/<name>#<pointer>), not by sourceDescriptions.

etag.description: On an attach response: the working copy's NEW concurrency token (the attach bumped it; present the fresh etag on the next save). List entries omit it.

AttachedSourceList.description: A working copy's attachments (unpaged — a workflow references few sources).

AttachSourceRequest.description: Attach (or replace) the source for a sourceDescriptions name: EXACTLY ONE of sourceName (a registry reference) or document (an inline upload/fetch result) — both or neither is rejected (400).

sourceName.description: Attach the registered source with this name (resolved at read time, reach-checked).

document.description: Attach this document inline (stored on the working copy verbatim).

type.description: The inline document's type (openapi | asyncapi | arazzo | jsonschema); detected from the document's own top-level field when omitted (a JSON Schema document declares none, so pass jsonschema explicitly).

SourceOperationDescriptor.description: One entry in a source's operation surface: an OpenAPI operation (operationId / path+method) or an AsyncAPI operation (channelPath+action), with RAW JSON Schema request/parameter/response shapes — exactly what the designer's typed editors and operation templates consume.

action.description: AsyncAPI: 2.x publish/subscribe map to receive/send exactly as the code generator maps them.

request.description: The request body (OpenAPI, the JSON content type preferred) or message payload (AsyncAPI).

schema.description: Raw JSON Schema, local $refs inlined to a bounded depth.
schema.title: RawSchema

request.title: SourceOperationRequestDescriptor

responses.description: The documented responses, keyed by status code (200, 4XX, default, …) — the key set drives the designer's criteria/failure-action templates.
responses.title: SourceOperationResponses

OperationSurface.description: A source document's full operation surface, in document order.

FetchSourceRequest.description: A server-side fetch of an OpenAPI/AsyncAPI/Arazzo document from a web endpoint (workflow-designer design §4.4): avoids browser CORS entirely, and an optional credential REFERENCE reuses the source-credential machinery for authenticated spec endpoints (the control plane resolves the reference; secret material never rides this API). The fetch validates and returns the document — it does not register or attach it; the caller does that with the result. Deployments restrict the scheme to https (an explicit deployment opt-in permits http) and should fence outbound destinations per their network policy.

url.description: The document URL (https; http only when the deployment opts in).

credential.title: FetchCredentialReference
credential.description: Authenticate the fetch with a registered source credential, referenced by its (sourceName, environment) key — reach-checked, non-disclosing (404 when absent or outside the caller's reach).

FetchedSourceDocument.description: The fetched, validated document plus what the control plane detected about it. JSON and YAML endpoints both work — YAML parses to the JSON form returned here.

type.description: Detected from the document's own top-level field.

version.description: The declared spec version (the value of that top-level field).

digest.description: SHA-256 (lower-hex) over the document's RFC 8785 canonical form — the same convention as catalog package hashes; compare digests to detect drift between fetches.

contentType.description: The upstream response's content type, when it sent one.

SourceCredentialKind.description: The credential SHAPE the resolved secret(s) build into a provider (non-sensitive metadata) — how a secret becomes an authenticator, not where it is applied. For an OpenAPI source the header kinds attach per request; for an AsyncAPI (channel) source the same kinds describe the broker credential presented at connect (ADR 0051): 'bearer' = one opaque secret presented at connection (a NATS token, an Azure Service Bus SAS); 'basic' = username + password (NATS user/pass, Kafka SASL/PLAIN or SCRAM, with the mechanism as non-secret config); 'oauth2ClientCredentials' = a client-credential exchange (Kafka OAUTHBEARER, Entra); 'mtls' = a client certificate at the TLS handshake. A channel binding also carries the environment's broker URL as non-secret config ('serverUrl', the channel analogue of the HTTP 'baseUrl' override); the transport protocol comes from the source document's servers[].protocol, never from the binding. 'mtls' — and EVERY kind on a channel binding — is connection-scoped (the credential authenticates the deployment's connection, not a run) and so cannot be usage-scoped to an individual run.

CredentialStatus.description: The derived health of a binding's referenced secret (§13.2), computed from expiresAt against the current time — never persisted. 'valid' = not near expiry (or no known expiry); 'expiringSoon' = within the rotation-warning window; 'expired' = past expiry.

SecretReference.description: A named, role-tagged reference to one secret in the external store. The ref is a pointer (scheme://locator[#version]) — never secret material.

name.description: The role this secret plays (e.g. value, password, clientSecret).

ref.description: The secret reference: scheme://locator[#version] (keyvault, awssm, vault, env, file).

CredentialConfigEntry.description: A single non-secret auth configuration value.

key.description: The configuration key (e.g. headerName, tokenUrl, scopes, username).

CredentialSecurityTag.description: A security label (KVP) scoping a binding for row authorization (§14.2) — e.g. tenant=acme, team=ops.

CredentialUsageGrant.description: An identity grant of which runs may USE a binding (§13). The deployment maps it to an unforgeable internal tag a run carries (e.g. {workflow, nightly-reconcile} → sys:workflow=nightly-reconcile) — usage cannot be self-granted by free-form tags.

value.description: The identity value (e.g. the base workflow id, or a tenant id).

CredentialUsageGrantee.description: The resolved grantee whose runs may USE a binding (§13/§16.5.4): its identity as the deployment-mapped {dimension,value} grants the deployment resolves to unforgeable internal tags, plus the resolved kind/label for display. Mirrors AdministratorGrant. A run may use the binding only if it carries every tag of this identity (label-superset). Omit on a binding to default to the creating principal's identity (the owner's runs).

identity.description: The grantee's resolved identity as {dimension,value} grants (a multi-tag grantee — e.g. a person — yields several, AND-matched).

label.description: An optional human-friendly display label for the resolved grantee.

CredentialBindingCreate.description: A source credential binding to create — references and non-secret metadata only. Secret material must never appear here; each secretRefs entry is a SecretRef pointer the runner dereferences.

secretRefs.description: The named references to secret material in the external store (at least one).

config.description: Non-secret auth configuration. Secret material must never appear here.

managementTags.description: Security tags scoping who may MANAGE this binding (§14.2), independent of the usage grant (usageGrantee). The deployment additionally stamps the creating principal's internal tenant tag, and rejects a set the creator's own reach does not admit (no privilege escalation). The reserved internal-tag prefix is not allowed here.

expiresAt.description: When the referenced long-lived secret expires, if knowable (e.g. a cert NotAfter, an API-key/refresh-token lifetime). Non-sensitive lifecycle metadata (§13.2) driving the derived credentialStatus; omit when expiry is unknown (treated as non-expiring).

rotatedAt.description: When the referenced secret was last rotated, if known. Non-sensitive lifecycle metadata for operator visibility; distinct from lastUpdatedAt.

CredentialBindingUpdate.description: Replacement references and non-secret metadata for an existing binding (its sourceName/environment identity is taken from the path and is immutable). An administrator may re-tag the managementTags reach scope; the deployment's internal tags are preserved.

managementTags.description: Replacement non-internal security tags scoping who may MANAGE this binding (§14.2); absent leaves the tags unchanged. The reserved internal-tag prefix is rejected (400); the deployment's internal tags are preserved.

expiresAt.description: When the (rotated) referenced secret expires, if knowable. Non-sensitive lifecycle metadata (§13.2); omit when unknown.

rotatedAt.description: When the referenced secret was last rotated, if known. Non-sensitive lifecycle metadata for operator visibility.

CredentialBindingSummary.description: A persisted source credential binding — references and non-secret metadata only. No secret material is ever included.

managementTags.description: Security tags scoping who may MANAGE this binding (§14.2), independent of the usage grant (usageGrantee).

expiresAt.description: When the referenced secret expires, if known (§13.2). Absent when expiry is unknown (non-expiring).

rotatedAt.description: When the referenced secret was last rotated, if known (§13.2).

CredentialBindingList.description: A page of source credential bindings, ordered by sourceName then environment.

nextPageToken.description: An opaque token to fetch the next page, or null/absent if this is the last page.

AdministratorIdentity.description: An administrator identity for a base workflow id (§15), named as the deployment-mapped grant the deployment resolves to an unforgeable internal identity (e.g. {workflow, nightly-reconcile} -> sys:workflow=nightly-reconcile). Administration cannot be self-granted by free-form tags.

value.description: The identity value (e.g. the base workflow id, or a tenant id).

AdministratorMemberWrite.description: Names an administrator to add (§15). Provide EITHER a resolved grantee from the picker — its `kind`, searchable `value`, and full `identity` (the {dimension,value} grants of GET /identity/grantees), which names a multi-tag grantee (e.g. a person resolved to {sys:tenant, sys:sub}) exactly — OR, for the simple case, a single deployment-mapped {dimension, value} grant (the kind is inferred from the dimension). When `identity` is present and non-empty it is the stored administrator identity.

value.description: The grantee value — the searched key (resolved-grantee form) or the single grant's value.

identity.description: The grantee's full resolved identity as {dimension, value} grants (from GET /identity/grantees). When present and non-empty this is the administrator identity; `kind` should accompany it.

label.description: An optional human-friendly display label, recorded for the §16.5.4 typeahead.

complete.description: Whether `identity` is the grantee's whole stamped identity (the picker's honest `complete`); defaults to true for the resolved-grantee form.

dimension.description: The single-grant identity dimension (the simple form; ignored when `identity` is provided). The kind is inferred from it.

AdministratorGrant.description: A recorded administrator of a base workflow id (§15), as a resolved identity (§16.5.4): a stable `digest` of its identity (the key for removal), the `identity` as {dimension,value} grants, and the optional resolved `kind`/`label` for display. Membership is exact set-equality on the identity; a single multi-tag grantee (e.g. a person) is one grant, not several.

digest.description: A stable opaque digest of this administrator's identity — the key for DELETE /administrators/{baseWorkflowId}/members/{digest}.

identity.description: The administrator's resolved identity as {dimension,value} grants (a multi-tag grantee yields several entries within this one grant).

label.description: An optional human-friendly display label for the resolved grantee.

AdministratorList.description: The administrators of a base workflow id (§15), each a resolved identity with a stable digest for removal.

BroadeningOverlapGrantee.description: An existing grantee whose resolved identity the just-granted (narrower) identity STRICTLY contains (§16.5.4 membership) — the grant broadens administration to also admit this grantee, and everyone whose identity contains the grant. Reach-filtered to grantees the caller may see.

value.description: The grantee's subject value (e.g. the person/team/role name).

label.description: An optional human-friendly display label for the grantee.

BroadeningAdvisory.description: A NON-BLOCKING advisory (§16.5.4): the identity just granted is broader than one or more existing grantees, so it confers administration on every principal whose identity contains it, not only the named grantees. Present on an add-administrator response only when such an overlap exists (reach-filtered to what the caller may see); the grant itself still succeeds. Compare the hard-blocking identity-collision (409), which refuses an ambiguous set-equal grant.

message.description: A human-readable explanation that the granted identity is broader than the listed existing grantees.

subsumesGrantees.description: The existing grantees the granted identity strictly contains (reach-visible to the caller); the grant broadens administration to also admit each of them.

AdministratorSetWrite.description: The replacement administrator set for a transfer (§15) — at least one identity; duplicates are coalesced.

EnvironmentSecurityTag.description: A security label (KVP) scoping an environment for row authorization (§14.2) — e.g. tenant=acme.

EnvironmentCreate.description: A deployment environment to create (design §7.7). Creating it grants the calling principal administration of it. The name is the stable identity; managementTags scope who may manage and see it (the deployment additionally stamps the creator's internal tenant tag and rejects a set the creator's reach does not admit).

name.description: The environment's stable name and identity (e.g. production, staging, DEV-MWA-1); credentials and runs key on it.

displayName.description: An optional human-friendly display name; defaults to the name when absent.

requireEvidence.description: Whether promotion into this environment requires green publish evidence (workflow-designer design §4.6): when true, a version may be made available here only if its server-attested scenario suite passed at publish. Absent/false preserves the default behaviour — unevidenced versions may be promoted.

allowsDraftRuns.description: Development-class flag (workflow-designer design §18): when true, working-copy DRAFTS may execute here as debug runs, using this environment's credential bindings. Absent/false — only published, availability-granted versions run here.

managementTags.description: Security tags (§14.2) scoping who may MANAGE and SEE this environment. The reserved internal-tag prefix is not allowed here.

EnvironmentUpdate.description: Replacement mutable metadata for an existing environment (its name and created-* audit are immutable). An administrator may re-tag the managementTags reach scope; the deployment's internal tags are preserved.

displayName.description: An optional human-friendly display name; defaults to the name when absent.

requireEvidence.description: Replacement promotion-readiness requirement (workflow-designer design §4.6); absent leaves the environment's requirement unchanged.

allowsDraftRuns.description: Replacement draft-run permission (workflow-designer design §18); absent leaves the environment's setting unchanged.

managementTags.description: Replacement non-internal security tags (§14.2) scoping who may MANAGE and SEE this environment; absent leaves the tags unchanged. The reserved internal-tag prefix is rejected (400); the deployment's internal tags are preserved.

EnvironmentSummary.description: A persisted deployment environment (design §7.7): a first-class, governed, reach-scoped resource.

requireEvidence.description: Whether promotion into this environment requires green publish evidence (workflow-designer design §4.6). Absent means false — unevidenced versions may be promoted.

allowsDraftRuns.description: Whether working-copy drafts may execute here as debug runs (workflow-designer design §18). Absent means false.

managementTags.description: Security tags scoping who may MANAGE and SEE this environment (§14.2).

EnvironmentList.description: A page of deployment environments, ordered by name.

nextPageToken.description: An opaque token to fetch the next page, or null/absent if this is the last page.

SourceSecurityTag.description: A security label (KVP) scoping a source for row authorization (§14.2) — e.g. tenant=acme.

SourceType.description: The kind of document a source registers (design §7.6): an OpenAPI description for an HTTP API source, an AsyncAPI description for a messaging source, or a JSON Schema document that workflow inputs schemas reference by external $ref (schemas/<name>#<pointer>). A jsonschema source is never a sourceDescription (the Arazzo spec pins that enum) — it attaches to a working copy alongside the declared sources and resolves through the standard registered-document mechanism.

SourceDocument.description: The registered OpenAPI/AsyncAPI document for the source, stored and returned verbatim. The control plane does not interpret it beyond reach scoping; clients read components.securitySchemes from it to derive credential auth (§7.5). Returned only on single-source reads, never in the list.

SourceCreate.description: A source to register (design §7.6). The name is the stable identity workflows reference in sourceDescriptions; managementTags scope who may see and manage it (the deployment additionally stamps the creator's internal tenant tag and rejects a set the creator's reach does not admit).

name.description: The source's registered name; must match the workflow sourceDescriptions entry that references it.

displayName.description: An optional human-friendly display name; defaults to the name when absent.

managementTags.description: Security tags (§14.2) scoping who may MANAGE and SEE this source. The reserved internal-tag prefix is not allowed here.

SourceUpdate.description: Replacement metadata for an existing source (its name, type, and created-* audit are immutable). An administrator may re-tag the managementTags reach scope; the deployment's internal tags are preserved. The document is optional: omit it to keep the registered document unchanged (e.g. to rename without re-uploading), or supply a new one to rotate it.

displayName.description: An optional human-friendly display name; defaults to the name when absent.

managementTags.description: Replacement non-internal security tags (§14.2) scoping who may MANAGE and SEE this source; absent leaves the tags unchanged. The reserved internal-tag prefix is rejected (400); the deployment's internal tags are preserved.

SourceSummary.description: A registered source without its document (design §7.6): the list projection. Fetch a single source to read its document.

managementTags.description: Security tags scoping who may MANAGE and SEE this source (§14.2).

Source.description: A registered source including its OpenAPI/AsyncAPI document (design §7.6): the single-source projection returned by register/get/update.

managementTags.description: Security tags scoping who may MANAGE and SEE this source (§14.2).

SourceList.description: A page of registered sources, ordered by name. Entries omit the document.

nextPageToken.description: An opaque token to fetch the next page, or null/absent if this is the last page.

AvailabilityEntry.description: One cell of the availability matrix (design §7.8): a workflow version made available in a deployment environment.

baseWorkflowId.description: The base workflow id the version belongs to.

environment.description: The deployment environment the version is available in.

createdBy.description: The actor that made the version available.

createdAt.description: When the version was made available.

AvailabilityList.description: A page of availability matrix entries (design §7.8) — either the environments a version is available in, or the versions available in an environment.

nextPageToken.description: An opaque token to fetch the next page, or null/absent if this is the last page.

GranteeKind.description: A well-known kind of grantee the deployment can resolve to a sys: identity (design §16.5.4).

ResolvedGrantee.description: A grantee resolved to its exact deployment-stamped identity (design §16.5.4): name it in a view/operate/administer grant and the membership match (exact set-equality) is guaranteed. The identity is given as {dimension,value} grants, never raw sys: tags.

value.description: The grantee value (a subject id, tenant/role name, or workflow id) — the prefix-searched key.

identity.description: The grantee's exact sys: identity as {dimension,value} grants.

source.description: Where this grantee was resolved: the store-indexed observed-identity typeahead, the external directory, or a validated free-typed value.

complete.description: Whether the identity is the principal's full stamped identity (true), or a best-effort single-tag mapping of a free-typed value that may not match if the deployment stamps finer (false).

GranteeList.description: A page of resolved grantees, ordered by (value, kind).

nextPageToken.description: An opaque token to fetch the next page, or null/absent if this is the last page.

IdentityCapabilities.description: What the deployment can resolve for the grantee pickers (design §16.5.4).

granteeKinds.description: The grantee kinds the deployment can resolve (empty when unscoped).

directorySearch.description: Whether an external directory is configured for live search (beyond the store-indexed observed-identity typeahead).

WhoAmI.description: The caller's own deployment-stamped identity as {dimension,value} grants (design §16.5.4); empty when unscoped.

AccessRequestSubmit.description: A request for elevated, time-bound access to a workflow (design §16.5). The requesting subject is taken from the caller, not this body.

baseWorkflowId.description: The base workflow id to request access to.

requestedScopes.description: The capability scopes requested (e.g. runs:write). An approval grants at most these, capped to run access.

requestedDurationSeconds.description: The proposed time-bound duration in seconds; capped at the deployment maximum. Absent defaults to the maximum.

AccessRequestView.description: An access request and its decision state (design §16.5).

subjectClaimType.description: The claim type the requester is identified by (and the grant keys on).

reason.description: The optional justification supplied with the request.

requestedDurationSeconds.description: The proposed time-bound duration in seconds, if any.

status.description: The lifecycle state. 'Eligible' (§16.5.3) means the request was approved as durable eligibility — the requester may self-elevate this JIT — rather than as a live grant ('Approved').

decidedAt.description: When the request was decided, if decided.

decisionReason.description: An optional note recorded with the decision.

grantedBindingId.description: The id of the security-policy binding that backs the grant ('Approved') or the eligibility assignment ('Eligible') (audit).

grantedUntil.description: When the granted entitlement (or, for 'Eligible', the eligibility window) expires; absent for a standing grant/eligibility.

AccessRequestList.description: A keyset page of access requests (design §16.5), oldest first by (createdAt, id).

nextPageToken.description: An opaque token to fetch the next page, or null/absent if this is the last page.

AccessRequestDecisionNote.description: An optional note recorded with an approve/deny/withdraw/revoke decision (design §16.5).

reason.description: An optional note explaining the decision.

AccessRequestSettlement.description: The decision the bootstrapped approval workflow enacts through settleAccessRequest: which terminal state to move the pending request to, plus the approver's optional note (design §16.5.1).

outcome.description: The decided outcome to enact: 'approved' writes the capped grant; 'eligible' writes standing eligibility; 'rejected' marks the request Denied; 'withdrawn' marks it Withdrawn.

reason.description: An optional note explaining the decision, carried from the approver.

AccessRequestEligibilityNote.description: Options for approving a request as durable eligibility (design §16.5.3).

reason.description: An optional note explaining the decision.

eligibilityWindowSeconds.description: How long the eligibility itself lasts, in seconds; absent means standing eligibility. Each activation is independently capped at the deployment maximum TTL.

AvailabilityRequestSubmit.description: A request to make a workflow version available in an environment (design §7.8). The requesting identity is taken from the caller, not this body.

baseWorkflowId.description: The base workflow id whose version to make available.

versionNumber.description: The version number to make available.

environment.description: The target environment to make the version available in.

AvailabilityRequestView.description: An availability request and its decision state (design §7.8).

reason.description: The optional justification supplied with the request.

status.description: The lifecycle state. 'Approved' means the version was made available in the environment; 'Withdrawn' means the requester withdrew the pending request.

decidedAt.description: When the request was decided, if decided.

decisionReason.description: An optional note recorded with the decision.

AvailabilityRequestList.description: A keyset page of availability requests (design §7.8), oldest first by (createdAt, id).

nextPageToken.description: An opaque token to fetch the next page, or null/absent if this is the last page.

AvailabilityRequestDecisionNote.description: An optional note recorded with an approve/deny/withdraw decision (design §7.8).

reason.description: An optional note explaining the decision.
